title: The Stolen Keys
scenario_intro: The keys to the storage room went missing from the front desk. They were there at 10 AM when the secretary left for a short break and were gone by 11 AM. Three people could have taken them: Alex (maintenance), Sam (the secretary), and Jordan (the intern). As the prosecutor, you must find out who had access to the keys and who took them.
hints_intro: To win you need to show who had the keys last and who was at the front desk when they went missing. Look for: when the keys were last seen, who was at the desk alone, and who was seen near the storage room.

characters.name: Alex
characters.persona: Maintenance worker. Used the keys at 10 AM to get into the storage room and says they returned them to the desk.
facts: Alex used the keys at 10:00 AM and returned them to the desk by 10:10 AM.
Sam left for break at 10:15 and returned at 10:45; the keys were gone when Sam returned.
Alex saw Jordan near the storage room around 10:35 AM.

characters.name: Sam
characters.persona: Front-desk secretary. Left for a break at 10:15 and returned at 10:45. The keys were gone when they got back.
facts: The keys were on the desk at 10:00 AM when Sam left for break.
Sam left at 10:15 and returned at 10:45; the keys were gone when Sam returned.
Jordan was at the front desk alone from 10:15 to 10:45 while Sam was on break.

characters.name: Jordan
characters.persona: Intern. Was covering the front desk while Sam was on break. Fidgets when asked about the keys.
characters.lie_strategy: Claim they didn't touch the keys; say they didn't notice they were gone until Sam asked.
facts: The keys were on the desk when Sam left at 10:15.
Jordan was alone at the front desk from 10:15 to 10:45.
The keys were reported missing at 11:00 AM.

truth.timeline: Jordan took the keys from the desk at 10:30 while alone, used them to get supplies, then lost them near the storage room.
truth.motive: Wanted to get something from storage without asking; then misplaced the keys.
truth.method: Took keys from desk when Sam was on break, used them, dropped them in the hallway.

evidence.description: Sam confirms the keys were on the desk at 10 AM when they left for their break and were gone when they returned at 10:45.
evidence.hint: The secretary's account of when the keys were last seen on the desk.
evidence.clue_instruction: Talk to Sam. Ask when the keys were on the desk, when they left for break, or when they noticed the keys were missing.

evidence.description: Alex used the keys at 10 AM and returned them to the desk by 10:10; Sam saw them there before leaving at 10:15.
evidence.hint: Maintenance returned the keys before the secretary left; keys were there at 10:15.
evidence.clue_instruction: Talk to Alex or Sam. Ask when Alex used the keys, when they returned them, or where the keys were at 10:15.

evidence.description: Jordan was alone at the front desk from 10:15 to 10:45; no one else was there during that time.
evidence.hint: The intern was the only person at the desk when the keys went missing.
evidence.clue_instruction: Talk to Sam. Ask who was at the desk during their break, or who was covering the front desk.

evidence.description: Sam says when they returned at 10:45 Jordan was at the desk and said they hadn't seen the keys—but Jordan had been there the whole time.
evidence.hint: The intern was at the desk the whole time the keys were there and when they went missing.
evidence.clue_instruction: Talk to Sam again. Ask what Jordan said when they asked about the keys, or whether Jordan was there the whole break.

evidence.description: Alex saw Jordan near the storage room at around 10:35—after Alex had already returned the keys.
evidence.hint: Someone saw the intern near the storage room during the time the keys went missing.
evidence.clue_instruction: Talk to Alex. Ask if they saw anyone near the storage room after they returned the keys, or who was in the hallway.
